title: phaseolus acutifolius var. tenuifolius
intro: Phaseolus acutifolius var. tenuifolius, also known as the Narrowleaf Bush Bean or tepary bean, is a type of wild bean native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and infections. The health benefits of Phaseolus acutifolius var. tenuifolius include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, reduce blood pressure, and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
